blockingIssues.description: The new i18n bootstrap hardcodes `lng: "zh"` in `src/i18n/index.ts`, which bypasses `i18next-browser-languagedetector` during init. That means the saved language in `skills-manage-language` is ignored on reload, so the Settings language switcher does not actually persist the user's choice across sessions.

The translation sweep is incomplete: runtime copy is still hard-coded in `CollectionView`, `CentralSkillsView`, `SkillDetail`, `CentralSkillCard`, and `Sidebar`. English mode still surfaces Chinese install/uninstall toasts, while Chinese mode still leaks English delete/refresh/install failure copy and accessibility text, so the milestone has not fully translated all user-facing strings.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the scrutiny validator/reviewer procedure so Task-spawned `scrutiny-feature-reviewer` subagents can run with automatic execution approval in this worker environment, or document the manual-review fallback explicitly.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: In this ui-overhaul scrutiny run, all three Task invocations for `ui-green-theme-and-visual-polish`, `ui-platform-icons-and-grid-layout`, and `ui-i18n-setup` exited immediately with `Exec ended early: insufficient permission to proceed`, so the validator had to inspect the handoffs, transcript skeletons, and commit diffs manually instead of using reviewer subagents.
